citation_note: Step language below is written for this simulation and is not a verbatim reproduction of any standard's text. Citations name the real regulatory framework governing the topic at the level of specificity we can stand behind (standard name / section subject), not invented clause numbers. OISD (Oil Industry Safety Directorate, Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas, Government of India) publishes OISD-STD-105 'Work Permit System', which is the standard governing permit issuance, suspension, and hot-work control referenced throughout. The Factories Act, 1948 sections cited are the well-established statutory provisions on dangerous fumes and fire precautions applicable to Indian process plants.

procedures.title: Gas Leak / Toxic-Flammable Atmosphere Response
procedures.source: OISD-STD-105 (Work Permit System) — permit suspension provisions; Factories Act 1948, Section 36 (Precautions against dangerous fumes, gases, etc.)
steps: Suspend all active permits in the affected zone immediately — no new hot or cold work may begin
Sound the zone evacuation alert and account for all personnel via muster point roll call
Do not permit zone re-entry without appropriate respiratory PPE
Isolate the suspected source (valve/line) from outside the zone if it can be done safely
Increase ventilation or activate emergency exhaust where available to restore dispersion
Confirm gas detector readings return to the safe band before authorizing re-entry
Log the incident and complete root-cause review before any permit in the zone is reissued

procedures.title: Hot Work Permit Suspension (Ignition Risk During Process Excursion)
procedures.source: OISD-STD-105 (Work Permit System) — hot work permit provisions; Factories Act 1948, Section 38 (Precautions in case of fire)
steps: Immediately suspend the active hot-work permit and stop all spark- or flame-producing work
Remove hot-work equipment and other ignition sources from the zone
Verify fire watch personnel and extinguishing equipment are stationed before any resumption is considered
Do not reissue the permit until the pressure/temperature excursion is confirmed resolved and relief capacity is restored to 100%
Re-verify the gas-free / LEL certificate before hot work resumes
Document the near-miss and route the permit through the issuing authority for re-approval

procedures.title: Rotating Equipment Vibration / Bearing Alarm Response
procedures.source: OISD process safety management framework — mechanical integrity / predictive maintenance practice for rotating equipment
steps: Cross-check the vibration and bearing-temperature trend against baseline to confirm this is a genuine trend, not a sensor fault
Reduce load or bring the equipment to a controlled shutdown if the trend continues to climb
Escalate the overdue maintenance task to the planner for priority scheduling — do not defer it further
Perform a manual bearing and lubrication inspection before restart
Update the asset's maintenance record and shorten the inspection interval going forward
